rationale: All 4 delivered commits (35981fa P1, 66cbb0e P1b, ad3e937 P3, cceb66b P2) are present on feat/deals_unification and their diffs match the plan's Touchpoints table and the task's ground-truth description exactly — verified by direct git show/diff reads, not assumed. Every money AC (AC1-AC8, P1b-1..P1b-4, AC9, AC10, AC12) is proven by real Fully-Automated tests per the plan's own validate-contract (Gate: PASS, PVL re-run 17-07-26) and the charter's Known-Gap ban was never invoked for a money AC. Independently re-verified live in this session (not merely cited): (1) the two DO-NOT-TOUCH functions (computeDealDiscountCents, checkDealEligibility) have zero diff across the full 35981fa~1..cceb66b range; (2) the apps/mobile eligibility twin has zero diff across the same range; (3) exactly one migration (0014, additive-nullable, no backfill, no destructive DDL) exists across all 4 commits; (4) the resolver dispatch, the two new pure discount functions, the admin merged-state PATCH cross-validation, the mint-side generate guards, and the admin UI picker/block all read exactly as the plan and commit messages describe. Two adversarial review cycles (post-P1 Fable/xhigh review -> P1b; a second cycle inside the P2 commit itself, per its message, closing the legacy value-less-discount zero-and-burn path and the unclearable-benefit PATCH dead-end) already fed back into the shipped code rather than being left as findings only — this pack's own adversarial-validation.json re-derives and re-checks those scenarios against the FINAL code, not the pre-fix code. Known accepted gaps (offer usage-limit enforcement on the coupon path, no DB CHECK for reward_id/offer_id mutual exclusivity, a cosmetic reused reject message, mobile display copy) are all descoped in the plan's own Open Gaps / Risks tables with documented rationale, not silently dropped. AC13 (backlog-note filing + SPEC annotation) is legitimately Agent-Probe/deferred UPDATE PROCESS work and does not gate the money-path decision. This APPROVE is evidentiary only, based on static/structural verification plus cited prior live-run results (not an independent live suite re-run — that would require a local Postgres instance, out of scope for this pack-generation session) — mustStopBeforeFinalize remains true; the user's own review of this pack is still the actual finalize gate.
